findings.title: Unreadable or missing roots produce a successful zero-work backfill
findings.impact: A typo, deleted specs root, or permission problem returns an empty successful summary instead of failing, so operators can believe a repo-wide refresh completed when no packets were inspected.

findings.title: One malformed graph-metadata file aborts the entire batch
findings.impact: A single corrupt packet blocks refresh of every later packet in the sorted traversal and the summary cannot report partial success or per-folder failures.
findings.reproduction: Code inspection: runBackfill has no per-folder try/catch around loadGraphMetadata/derive/refresh; loadGraphMetadata throws on invalid content.

findings.title: Regression tests cover happy-path backfill but not failure paths
findings.impact: The behavior most likely to mislead operators, invalid roots, malformed metadata, and malformed CLI argument order, can regress without the scoped test file catching it.
findings.reproduction: Scoped vitest passes 3 tests, all focused on inclusion, writing, and active-only traversal.

findings.title: --root consumes the next flag when its value is missing
findings.impact: A malformed command such as --root --active-only scans a path literally named --active-only and silently drops activeOnly mode, returning a zero-work success.

findings.title: Traversal is synchronous and unbounded for operator-supplied roots
findings.impact: An accidental broad root can synchronously walk a very large tree without max-depth, max-folder, or elapsed-time guardrails. That is local operator DoS, not an external exploit.
findings.reproduction: Code inspection: walk recursively descends all non-excluded directories and only stops on readdir failure or traversal exhaustion.
